basic_info.description: A tech enthusiast and problem solver with a heart for people, I thrive at the intersection of code and connections. With a strong foundation in programming and a genuine love for understanding user needs, I'm dedicated to crafting meaningful and impactful experiences✨. I enjoy working on projects - whether I'm on the technical or non-technical side of things - and especially love building for humans💛. If you're interested in learning more about me, feel free to explore the rest of my portfolio. For a quick overview, you can also check out my resume or CV below. If you have any questions or would like to get in touch, please don't hesitate to contact me – I'd love to connect!

projects.title: ReCollect
projects.description: With dozens of social media apps, it can be a difficult and boring task to collect and organize all of your media in the same place for easy sharing. ReCollect streamlines this process by offering an accessible platform to house and sort media from other platforms in a familiar social media environment that encourages frequent inter-user media sharing with a proper citation system.

projects.title: KARGO
projects.description: A personal project, KARGO was one of the most expanded application I had opportunity to work with. I've learned many technologies and solidified my skills in technoligies in which I had prior knowledge. The KARGO app allows people to get anything delivered no matter how big or small. The app allows users to see nearby KARGO kars and place KARGO delivery orders. Additionally, the app implements the Stripe payment API to allow users to pay for their orders.

projects.title: KARGO Driver
projects.description: A personal project, the KARGO driver app was aprojects that gave me the most insigt into AWS technology and how realtime connections between apps worked. The app allows KARGO drivers to recieve and accept delivery requests, and assists them with their navigation to the pickup and dropoff points.

projects.title: IBSA Website
projects.description: The IBSA Website is a website created for the Indonesian Bruin Association at UCLA in order to create an easy and efficient method for the club to communicate with it's 150+ members. was one of the first websites where I was able to take on the Front-end development role. It was a project in which I worked with a team and had to fulfill the request of the customer and deliver a satisfactory project. Through this project, I got to not only further my reactJS skills but was also able to learn about the development process and how to deliver a product to a customer.

projects.title: Discoveroo
projects.description: In order to learn about front-end development, I decided to buid a website from scratch. With the basics of reactJS and bootstrap, I was able to build Discoveroo a platform to find tutors, babysitters, cleaners, and other household help. Through this project I was also able to gain exposure to design. The idea initially begin as an app, and my first step was to create a figma prototype of the app which I then used to base the website off of. When I eventually switched to developing a website. I used adobe photoshop and aftereffects to create the designs on the login page and the logo.

more.title: CodeHERs
more.description: Passionate about bridging the gender gap in tech, I founded CodeHERs—a small effort with big dreams—to provide a supportive and empowering space for girls to explore the world of tech. Together, we're stitching up the gender gap, thread by thread.

more.title: Baturetno
more.description: Through engagement with the local community of Baturetno, Indonesia, I actively participated in initiatives that provided tangible support, offering me firsthand insight into the challenges faced by underdeveloped communities. This awareness of global disparities has always fueled my dedication to fostering positive change.

more.title: Proud Bruin
more.description: Double majored in Computer Science and Business Economics at UCLA. From coding to crunching numbers, I got the best of both worlds under the sunny skies of LA. Go Bruins!

more.title: Outdoor Addict
more.description: When I'm not behind a screen, you'll most likely find me out and about, soaking in the great outdoors. In my opinion, there's no better way to hit the reset button than by walking up and down a really big hill :)

more.title: IBSA
more.description: I managed allocation of finances and came up with cost effective solutions as Finance Officer for UCLA's Indonesian Bruin Student Association (IBSA) in order to plan events to help build and cultivate UCLA's Indonesian community of over 150 students.
